markdown:
#  Retrieve a Deleted Change Event 
##  Background 
If you have moved a change event to the Recyle Bin (see [Delete a Change Event](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/delete-a-change-event)), you can restore it with the click of a button using the steps below.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Standard' level permissions or higher on the project's Change Events tool.


##  Prerequisites 
  * You must have at least one change event in the Recycle Bin. See [Delete a Change Event](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/delete-a-change-event).


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to your project's **Change Events** tool.
  2. Click the **Recycle Bin** tab.
  3. Locate the change event to restore. Then click **Recover**. ![Recover Change Event](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/2a8d7704559ce282f9362409a050fde5/ea7106039ae4d6aed103b176917e129b/change-events-recycle-bin.png?fm=webp)


## See Also
  * [Delete a Change Event](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/delete-a-change-event)
  * [Delete Change Events](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/delete-change-events)
  * [Change Events - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/permissions)
title: Retrieve a Deleted Change Event
description: How to retrieve a change event that has been deleted so that it appears in the Change Event log page.
